release_date_definition: First public developer availability of the named upstream model through an official API or downloadable weights. For canonical model-family records, availability through an earlier preview or experimental identifier counts; the exact identifiers and their kinds are listed separately. Private previews and announcements without access do not count.

identifier_type_definition.model: A canonical catalog record for a named API model or model family that is not itself a dated snapshot.
identifier_type_definition.snapshot: A canonical catalog record for a date-pinned API model; exact provider spelling is stored in identifiers.
identifier_type_definition.weights: A canonical catalog record for a named downloadable model-weight release; exact repository identifiers are stored in identifiers.

identifier_kind_definition.model: A fixed official identifier that is neither a rolling alias nor a dated snapshot.
identifier_kind_definition.alias: An official identifier that may resolve to a newer underlying model over time.
identifier_kind_definition.snapshot: An immutable official identifier pinned to a particular model version.
identifier_kind_definition.weights: An official identifier for downloadable model weights.

availability_definition.api: Public access through an official developer API.

availability_stage_definition.public_preview: Public developer access labeled preview or experimental by the provider.
availability_stage_definition.public: Public developer access without a preview or experimental label.

date_precision_definition.day: The event date is known to the calendar day.
date_precision_definition.month: Only the event month is known; normalized compatibility dates use its first day.
date_precision_definition.year: Only the event year is known; normalized compatibility dates use January 1.

lifecycle_status_definition.active: The provider documented the scoped model, channel, or identifier as available on the observation date.
lifecycle_status_definition.deprecated: The provider announced or documented that support is ending, but access may continue.
lifecycle_status_definition.retired: The provider ended access through the scoped channel or identifier.
lifecycle_status_definition.retirement_scheduled: The provider published a future retirement date.

lifecycle_date_role_definition.announced: The date on which the provider announced the lifecycle change.
lifecycle_date_role_definition.effective: The date on which the lifecycle status took effect.
lifecycle_date_role_definition.observed: The date on which the current provider status was verified.

relationship_type_definition.snapshot_of: The record is an immutable snapshot of the target rolling or named model.
relationship_type_definition.alias_of: The record is an alias of the target model.

coverage.statement: This is a curated, non-exhaustive catalog. A missing model does not mean that the model or release does not exist.
coverage.provider_inclusion_criteria: Providers are included when they publish first-party model access and sufficiently precise primary evidence for developer availability.
coverage.model_inclusion_criteria: Records cover selected general-purpose text or multimodal models with public first-party API access or official downloadable weights. Private previews, fine-tunes, embeddings, media-only models, and third-party-only aliases are excluded unless explicitly added.
